top_stories.title: Phishing and MFA exploitation: Targeting the keys to the kingdom
top_stories.summary: A 2025 security analysis identifies increasing attacker focus on exploiting weaknesses in MFA workflows and using compromised credentials to launch phishing attacks from trusted accounts. The report centers on trust exploitation as a core theme, highlighting how valid credentials and MFA bypass techniques are being weaponized against organizations.

top_stories.title: Cross‑tenant helpdesk impersonation to data exfiltration: A human-operated intrusion playbook
top_stories.summary: Threat actors are conducting social engineering attacks via Microsoft Teams, impersonating IT helpdesk staff to trick users into granting remote access across tenant boundaries. The attack chain leverages legitimate administrative tools and standard protocols for lateral movement and data exfiltration, exploiting cross-tenant collaboration features in Microsoft's ecosystem.

top_stories.title: Vercel Employee's AI Tool Access Led to Data Breach
top_stories.summary: A Vercel employee's access to an AI tool resulted in a data breach via stolen OAuth tokens. Security researchers highlight OAuth tokens as an emerging critical attack surface enabling lateral movement in enterprise environments.

top_stories.title: Tycoon 2FA Phishers Scatter, Adopt Device Code Phishing
top_stories.summary: Threat actors behind the Tycoon 2FA phishing-as-a-service platform have shifted tactics to adopt device code phishing attacks. The technique exploits legitimate OAuth device authorization flows to bypass MFA, tricking victims into granting attackers access to their accounts without stealing credentials directly.

top_stories.title: Prove Identity Platform connects verification, authentication, and fraud prevention
top_stories.summary: Prove has launched the Prove Identity Platform, an integrated solution combining identity verification, authentication, and fraud prevention into a continuous real-time process. The platform is designed to handle identity for users, businesses, and AI agents, addressing authorization challenges as agentic commerce grows through partnerships like OpenAI and Stripe's Agentic Commerce Protocol.

news.title: Ransomware, fraud, and lawsuits drive cyber insurance claims to new peaks
news.summary: At-Bay's 2026 InsurSec Report, covering over 100,000 policy years of claims data, found cyber insurance claim frequency rose 7% year-over-year with average severity reaching $221,000 and ransomware severity hitting $508,000. Remote access services were the entry point for 87% of ransomware claims in 2025, pointing to access control weaknesses as a primary attack vector.

news.title: Cyberattack on French government agency triggers phishing alert
news.summary: France Titres (ANTS), the French government agency managing official identity documents including national ID cards, passports, and driver's licenses, disclosed a data breach detected on April 15. The breach exposed user data from its online portal, which handles systems for official identity and registration documents under the French Ministry of the Interior.

news.title: Toxic Combinations: When Cross-App Permissions Stack into Risk
news.summary: Researchers disclosed that Moltbook, a social network for AI agents, exposed 35,000 email addresses and 1.5 million agent API tokens across 770,000 active agents due to an open database. The breach also revealed plaintext third-party credentials, including OpenAI API keys, stored within private messages between agents.

news.title: No Exploit Needed: How Attackers Walk Through the Front Door via Identity-Based Attacks
news.summary: The article examines identity-based attacks as a primary breach vector, focusing on how attackers use stolen credentials rather than technical exploits to gain access. It covers credential stuffing and related techniques that bypass traditional security controls by authenticating through legitimate means.

news.title: CISA Adds 8 Exploited Flaws to KEV, Sets April-May 2026 Federal Deadlines
news.summary: CISA added eight vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog, including CVE-2023-27351, an improper authentication flaw in PaperCut, along with Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager flaws. Federal agencies face remediation deadlines in April-May 2026.

news.title: ‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
news.summary: Tyler Robert Buchanan, a senior member of the cybercrime group Scattered Spider, pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft for his role in SMS phishing attacks in 2022. The attacks targeted at least a dozen major technology companies, with the group stealing tens of millions of dollars in cryptocurrency through credential theft campaigns.

news.title: Vercel Breach Tied to Context AI Hack Exposes Limited Customer Credentials
news.summary: Vercel disclosed a security breach originating from the compromise of third-party AI tool Context.ai, which was used by a Vercel employee. The attacker leveraged that access to take over the employee's Google Workspace account and gain unauthorized access to certain internal Vercel systems, exposing limited customer credentials.

news.title: Making opportunistic cyberattacks harder by design
news.summary: Microsoft published details on how it secures Dynamics 365 and Power Platform by removing credentials and reducing attack surfaces through platform engineering. The approach focuses on eliminating standing credentials and minimizing exposure to opportunistic threats as part of Microsoft's secure-by-design strategy.

news.title: Securely connecting on-premises data systems to Amazon Redshift with IAM Roles Anywhere
news.summary: AWS published a guide on using IAM Roles Anywhere to establish secure, private connections between on-premises data systems and Amazon Redshift. The solution eliminates the need for long-lived access keys by leveraging certificate-based authentication to grant temporary AWS credentials to workloads running outside of AWS.

news.title: AWS Managed Microsoft AD is now available on Windows functional level 2016
news.summary: AWS has upgraded all AWS Managed Microsoft AD directories to Windows functional level 2016, applied automatically to existing directories. This update to AWS Directory Service for Microsoft AD brings access to newer Active Directory features associated with the 2016 functional level.

news.title: [Webinar] Eliminate Ghost Identities Before They Expose Your Enterprise Data
news.summary: A webinar is being promoted focused on managing non-human identities (NHIs) such as service accounts, API tokens, and OAuth grants. The content references a statistic that compromised service accounts and forgotten API keys were behind 68% of cloud breaches in 2024, framing NHI governance as a critical IAM challenge.

news.title: Three Microsoft Defender Zero-Days Actively Exploited; Two Still Unpatched
news.summary: Threat actors are actively exploiting three zero-day vulnerabilities in Microsoft Defender, tracked as BlueHammer, RedSun, and UnDefend, to gain elevated privileges on compromised systems. Two of the three flaws remain unpatched, with Huntress issuing the warning about the ongoing exploitation activity.

news.title: Sam Altman's project World looks to scale its human verification empire. First stop: Tinder. | TechCrunch
news.summary: Sam Altman's World project is expanding its Orb-based human verification technology through new partnerships, with Tinder announced as an early partner. The initiative focuses on anonymous biometric verification to distinguish humans from AI, representing a novel approach to identity verification at scale.

news.title: Sam Altman's World Teams With Zoom, Tinder to Better Verify Humans in the AI Age
news.summary: Sam Altman's World (formerly Worldcoin) is expanding its iris-scanning human verification technology through new partnerships with Tinder for U.S. user verification and Zoom's Deep Face feature. The integrations use World's biometric identity platform to distinguish humans from AI in digital interactions.

news.title: Sam Altman’s World project launches major upgrade to fight deepfakes and bots
news.summary: Sam Altman's World project has launched a major upgrade aimed at combating deepfakes and bots. The project uses biometric iris-scanning orbs to verify human identity and distinguish real users from AI-generated identities or automated bots.

news.title: Sam Altman’s World releases major protocol upgrade, introduces World ID app
news.summary: Sam Altman's World project has released a major protocol upgrade and introduced the World ID app, a digital identity verification system. The product is designed to verify human authenticity in AI-driven environments using biometric-based identity credentials.

news.title: Two-Factor Authentication Breaks Free From the Desktop
news.summary: The article discusses extending two-factor authentication (2FA) beyond traditional desktop/IT environments to address physical-world security scenarios. The piece focuses on how threat actors bypass security systems outside conventional IT contexts and proposes 2FA as an additional barrier.

news.title: Microsoft, Salesforce Patch AI Agent Data Leak Flaws
news.summary: Microsoft and Salesforce patched prompt injection vulnerabilities in their respective AI agent platforms, Copilot and Agentforce, that could have allowed external attackers to exfiltrate sensitive data. The flaws relate to AI agent security rather than core IAM functionality, though they touch on authorization boundaries within AI-assisted enterprise tools.

news.title: Privilege Elevation Dominates Massive Microsoft Patch Update
news.summary: Microsoft released a massive patch update addressing 165 vulnerabilities, with privilege elevation bugs making up more than half of the total. Two zero-day vulnerabilities were included among the elevation-of-privilege flaws patched in the update.

news.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
news.summary: Russian military intelligence hackers are exploiting vulnerabilities in older routers to mass-harvest Microsoft Office authentication tokens across more than 18,000 networks. The campaign operates without deploying malware, instead silently siphoning authentication tokens to gain unauthorized access to targeted systems.
